source_documents: https://www.fatf-gafi.org (Trade-Based Money Laundering - Trends & Developments 2020; Risk Indicators 2021)
risk_signals: Transfers to or from high-risk or secrecy jurisdictions with no economic rationale
Funds moved through multiple countries or correspondent-banking hops before returning
Cross-border wire patterns inconsistent with the customer's business geography
analyst_reasoning: Cross-border layering exploits gaps between jurisdictions to break the audit trail. The laundering value is in the movement itself, so the diagnostic is transfers that lack a business reason, route through high-risk or secrecy jurisdictions, and add hops that serve only to obscure origin.
investigation_steps: Map the full cross-border path including correspondent-bank intermediaries
Score counterpart jurisdictions for AML risk and secrecy
Test each leg for a genuine underlying economic purpose
decision: File an STR, apply enhanced due diligence on the corridor, and coordinate with correspondent banks / FIU where cross-border cooperation is needed.
outcome: Multi-jurisdiction layering is among the hardest to trace; early corridor-level monitoring is the main defence.
lessons_learned: Movement without economic rationale is the essence of cross-border layering
Correspondent-banking hops can hide the true origin and must be mapped end to end
Jurisdiction risk scoring is central to prioritising cross-border alerts
